rulings.Case Title: BRUCE VANDERHOFF et al VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT AEROJET-GENERAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Aerojet-General Corporation's motion for summary judgment is continued to June 29, 2016 pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rule 2.7(B) for defendant's failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. Defendant must deliver courtesy copies of both its moving papers and its reply papers to Department 503 no later than 4:00 p.m. on June 23, 2016. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY SHAW ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC'S MOTION TO TRANSFER. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion to transfer venue to Sacramento County is denied. The motion is denied on the proper court ground because it is untimely. Defendant filed an answer and did not file a motion to transfer at that time. (C.C.P. §§ 396b(a), 397(a).) Even if the Court could consider a discretionary transfer pursuant to C.C.P. § 397(a), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that San Francisco is not the proper court. Finally, even assuming San Francisco is not the proper court, defendant may only move to change venue to its residence and defendant did not show that its residence is in Sacramento County. As to C.C.P. § 397(c), defendant failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted by transfer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARY LYONS VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 23, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANT BRENNTAG SPECIALTIES, INC'S Motion To Quash For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Specially appearing defendant Brenntag Specialties, Inc.'s motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction will be continued pursuant to plaintiffs' request to conduct jurisdictional discovery, which is granted. The parties must appear at the hearing at 9:30a.m. to discuss a supplemental briefing schedule and new hearing date. The discovery is limited to the issue of personal jurisdiction. Plaintiff's supplemental opposition, if any, will be limited to newly-discovered information obtained from the jurisdictional discovery. Supplemental reply, if any, will be limited to responding to the newly-discovered information addressed in the supplemental opposition. The parties must meet and confer prior to the hearing regarding the briefing schedule and hearing date. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. Plaintiff must bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(503/GLW)
